Check · Design Gap Inventory — comp vs live

Comp = NEW_CHECK_COMPS.dc.html / STYLE_GUIDE.md. Live = staging.checkitforme.com (checkit.html), captured 2026-07-07 via Playwright. Comp side rendered from exact CSS recipes; live side is real screenshots.

COMP SAYS = intendedSITE DOES = shipped✅ owner-approved divergence⚠️ no sign-off — your call
The skin that decides everythingColor tokensType scale (Inter)RadiiDepth / shadow recipesHome & brand themesPrimary CTALogin & verifyStore finder & rowsCall sheet & live callVerdict page (spec — not screenshotted)PlansAccount & toastsScores & content pagesFooterSpanish (i18n)Outbound copy (SMS / email / system)

0 · The skin that decides everything

Depth system (whole look)

⚠️ no sign-off — your call
COMP SAYS

The elevated / soft-depth skin: every surface is raised (vertical gradient + drop shadow + 1px inner top highlight), carved (flat dark + inset shadow), or glass (on verdict washes only). No hairline borders.

raised card
#2D2D34 → #27272D
carved well · #1B1B20
SITE DOES

Staging ships the flat v1 skin: near-black page, flat sheets, borders instead of depth. The elevated skin is fully built in checkit.html as html[data-skin="v2"] (368 rules) but the shipped default never sets that attribute.

flat card · #1A1A24
1px rgba border
Drift: The comp look already exists in code, just switched off. One decision — flip staging/prod to data-skin="v2" (comp wins) or bless v1 and rewrite the guide flat (site wins) — settles most rows below.

1 · Color tokens

Page background

COMP SAYS
#1D1D22page
SITE DOES
#0C0C12--bg
Drift: Site page is ~2 shades darker / flatter.

Card & sheet surface

COMP SAYS
row card (raised)
#26262Bbig card
SITE DOES
#1A1A24--sheet

flat fill, 1px rgba(255,255,255,.08) border

Drift: Comp cards are raised gradients with no border; site cards are one flat fill with a hairline border (§1 “no hairline borders” is violated).

Muted text

COMP SAYS
#8A8A96muted
SITE DOES
#6B6B7B--muted
Drift: Site muted text is dimmer/greyer.

Accent — per vertical (matches)

✅ owner-approved divergence
COMP SAYS
#FFCB05Pokémon
#E23636One Piece
#E4002BTopps
#EC4899NeeDoh
SITE DOES
#FFCB05injected --accent

One injected var drives logo, hero word, CTA halo. In agreement with the comp’s “one accent per vertical, green CTA stays constant.”

Drift: No drift — the per-vertical accent system matches. Kept here as the one clean pass.

Verdict tones

COMP SAYS
#4ADE80in
#EF4444out
#FBBF24unclear
#F59E0Brestock / over-retail
SITE DOES
#4ADE80in
#EF4444out
#FBBF24unclear
#F59E0Bsoon

washes #266440 / #6b2427 / #6c5419 / #6e490f → --bg

Drift: Verdict palette matches; only the wash fades to the darker --bg instead of #1D1D22.

Purple in product UI

COMP SAYS

Purple #A78BFA is board-chrome only (screen-label pills, DEV NOTE). §8: “no purple accents on UI.”

SITE DOES
#A78BFA--purple

used live for the store-finder CTA, store-selected tint, Runnr.

Drift: The comp keeps purple out of product; the site uses it as a functional accent (finder CTA below).

2 · Type scale (Inter)

Hero / verdict title

COMP SAYS
Pokémon in stock?

30px / 900 / −1px

SITE DOES

Hero H1 matches (30/900/−1px). But the verdict title renders 25px/900/−.6px vs comp 30/900/−1px.

Drift: Hero matches; the result-page verdict title is smaller on the site.

Roles (spec)

COMP SAYS
  • Modal title 19–21/900/−.4
  • Sheet/page title 20/800/−.3
  • Row/store name 14–15/700
  • CTA label 12.5–13/800 +.14em CAPS
  • Eyebrow 10–10.5/700 +.15em CAPS
SITE DOES
  • Modal title 19–21/900 ✓
  • Store name 14.5/700 ✓
  • CTA 16/900/−.2 (comp 12.5–13/800 CAPS)
  • OTP 26/800 .5em
Drift: Site CTA labels are larger, heavier, and not the tracked-caps eyebrow style the comp uses.

3 · Radii

Cards, sheets, pop-ups

COMP SAYS
  • big card / pop-up 26
  • sheet top 28
  • row card 13–14
  • tile/banner 16
  • pill/capsule/track 999
SITE DOES
  • sheets/cards cluster at 16
  • inputs/tints 12–14
  • pills 999 ✓
  • poll buttons 13
Drift: Site sheets/cards are less rounded (16 vs 26–28) — reads flatter/tighter than the comp’s soft cards.

4 · Depth / shadow recipes

Raised vs carved vs glass

⚠️ no sign-off — your call
COMP SAYS
raised · 0 8px 14px -8px / inset 1px top
carved · inset 0 2px 6px
glass (on wash only)
SITE DOES

The same raised/carved recipes exist verbatim — but only inside the dormant data-skin="v2". The shipped v1 uses flat fills + a border, so most surfaces read flat.

Drift: Recipes are correct in code; they just don’t paint because v2 is off (see row 0).

5 · Home & brand themes

Home · Pokémon

✅ owner-approved divergence
COMP SAYS

Hero word in accent, green primary CTA, S2 switcher (launch = Retail + Kiosk), footer strip. Accent #FFCB05.

SITE DOES

S2 labels render “Check a store · Kiosks” (comp: Retail·Kiosk). Finder CTA is purple.

Drift: Launch-nav reduction is approved; the finder CTA colour + label wording are the live deltas.

Home · One Piece

COMP SAYS

Accent #E23636, logo swap, hero “One Piece cards in stock?”.

SITE DOES

Accent + logo + copy swap correctly per vertical.

Drift: Per-vertical theming matches the comp system.

Home · Topps NBA

COMP SAYS

Accent #E4002B, logoScale .62, hero “Topps NBA cards in stock?”.

SITE DOES

Matches.

Drift: Theming matches.

Home · NeeDoh

COMP SAYS

Accent #EC4899, logoScale 1.5, hero “NeeDoh in stock?”.

SITE DOES

Matches.

Drift: Theming matches.

6 · Primary CTA

Capsule CTA

COMP SAYS

2.5px gradient ring around a dark capsule (#20202A), label 13/800 tracked caps + → arrow. Green ring = primary.

CHECK THIS STORE →
SITE DOES
Continue →
Find my stores

Login/primary = solid green fill; finder = purple pill (--ptint/--pline/--purple).

Drift: Comp = green ring capsule everywhere; site = solid-fill green + a purple finder pill. The ring version exists only in v2.

7 · Login & verify

Login pop-up

COMP SAYS

Centered pop-up (big card radius 26 over rgba(5,6,9,.66)), no grabber. Eyebrow “PHONE OR EMAIL”, accepts phone or email. Ring capsule CTA.

SITE DOES

Bottom-sheet-style card with a corner ×, phone-only (+1 flag), “First check’s on us!”, solid-green “Continue →”. No email path, no eyebrow.

Drift: Placement (sheet vs centered pop-up), phone-only vs phone/email, and the solid CTA all differ.

Verify code

COMP SAYS

6-digit code pop-up. §7: no env/staging chrome ever ships in UI.

SITE DOES

OTP 26/800 .5em, code pre-filled 000000 with a visible “⚙️ Staging. No real text sent.” hint (staging only).

Drift: Fine for staging; confirm the staging hint is stripped in prod (guide §7 / L1c).

8 · Store finder & rows

Finder + store rows

COMP SAYS

Carved search well, radius control, store rows = logo slot + name (700) + meta + right cell (miles / price / status icon chip). Chain PNG on #1F1F25; independents = embossed 2-letter tile — never blank.

SITE DOES

Green radius slider ✓, “BEST BETS”, rows with name/meta/miles. Several logo tiles render a broken-image placeholder.

Drift: Row anatomy matches; logo slots are failing to load a mark on some rows (should fall back to the embossed initials tile).

9 · Call sheet & live call

Call sheet (confirm)

COMP SAYS

Bottom sheet (rounded top, 40×5 grabber): store icon, product line in accent, big accent capsule CTA, charge note.

SITE DOES

Bottom sheet with store, “Pokémon · cuesta 1 verificación”, big CTA “Verifica esta tienda”. (Captured in ES — language persisted.)

Drift: Matches the comp’s bottom-sheet call confirm; CTA is solid-fill rather than ring.

Live call

COMP SAYS

Live phone icon with animated sound-wave arcs (ckWave), 2px timeline, streaming transcript (STAFF / CHECK-AI bubbles).

SITE DOES

Pulsing green ring, “Llamando a la tienda… / Está timbrando…”, step counter, mono transcript. (Simulated call didn’t reach a verdict in the capture window.)

Drift: Live-call composition matches. Final verdict page not captured — see gaps.

10 · Verdict page (spec — not screenshotted)

Verdict page

COMP SAYS

Wash from status bar → glass pills → glowing RESULT chip → verdict title (30/900) → price (34/900) → max two lines → green shine CTA → timeline → YOUR HUNT → restock → “N checks used” → footer.

SITE DOES

All verdict copy verified from source (13-status table, checkit.html ~4620–4660). Title renders 25/900. Verdict notes write “No charge.” literally (4640–4648) — guide says a green shield should carry that meaning.

Drift: Structure matches in v2; the shipped title is smaller and the “no charge” wording violates the copy rule. Needs a clean screenshot next pass.

11 · Plans

Plan tiers

✅ owner-approved divergence
COMP SAYS

Bottom sheet. Tiers use the muted-yellow selection ring (§5.5/§8: never a green ring next to a green CTA). Each tier led by a perk icon (🔔/📅/🎯/🗺).

Collector · muted-yellow ring
SITE DOES

“Pick your plan”, Monthly/Annual carved toggle, Family/Collector/Hunter/Operator. Selected tier uses a GREEN ring. No perk icons. Corner × present.

Drift: Selection colour (green vs muted-yellow) is a real drift; perk-icons are approved but not shipped; corner-× removal approved but not shipped.

12 · Account & toasts

Account sheet

COMP SAYS

Centered pop-up (My-checks screens = pop-ups, §5.11): checks-remaining rows + “RUN A CHECK” capsule + Sign out.

SITE DOES

Bottom sheet with balance, run-a-check CTA, Sign out. (Pop-up vs sheet placement.)

Drift: My-checks surfaces render as bottom sheets rather than centered pop-ups.

Bottom toast

COMP SAYS

Capsule that slides up, holds ~2.4s: green “✓ You’re in”, neutral “Link copied”, accent “1 check refunded”. 14.5/800, one at a time.

SITE DOES

“✓ You’re in” green toast on login — matches. Full toast set catalogued in copy extract (~40 strings).

Drift: Toast system matches the comp.

13 · Scores & content pages

Scores

COMP SAYS

Yellow trophy + 21/900 title + accent Post pill. Empty = dashed carved panel, “Be the first to post a score.”

SITE DOES

Scores surface present. Confirm empty-state dashed panel + Post capsule match.

Drift: Broadly matches; verify the empty-state chrome.

About

COMP SAYS

Content page opens as a bottom sheet.

SITE DOES

About renders as a sheet.

Drift: Content pages ship as sheets — consistent with site pattern.

FAQ

COMP SAYS

Content page.

SITE DOES

FAQ renders as a sheet.

Drift: OK.

Kiosks tab

COMP SAYS

S2 “Kiosk” vertical: crowd-built vending map, receipt = free check.

SITE DOES

Kiosks tab renders; empty-state copy “No kiosks mapped within {r} mi yet.”

Drift: Kiosk surface present.

14 · Footer

Footer

⚠️ no sign-off — your call
COMP SAYS

Two rows on the #17171C strip: ① links on ONE line — Scores About FAQ Contact Legal (Terms+Privacy = Legal) + EN pill at the row’s end. ② wordmark + ©2026 left, Discord/X circles right.

ScoresAboutFAQContactLegal · EN ▾
SITE DOES

Links read Scores About FAQ Contact Terms Privacy (split, no “Legal”). Cluster is centered; socials sit on their own row; EN pill bottom-right.

Drift: Terms/Privacy not collapsed to “Legal”; layout centered rather than left/right split (centering has no sign-off).

15 · Spanish (i18n)

ES home

COMP SAYS

Fixed UI lines hand-written (voice + tokens kept); same rules — no em-dash, fewest words. Spine ES: “¿en stock? Lo verificamos por ti.” / “Sin respuesta = sin cargo.”

SITE DOES

“Pokémon ¿en stock? Lo verificamos por ti.”, “Verificar una tienda / Kioscos”, “ENCUENTRA TU TIENDA”. Reads clean.

Drift: ES localisation matches the copy guide’s spine.

16 · Outbound copy (SMS / email / system)

“No charge” wording

COMP SAYS

A green shield carries “no charge” — don’t write the words.

SITE DOES

Verdict notes write “No charge.” literally (checkit.html:4640–4648).

Drift: Copy rule vs shipped strings — flag for the copy lane.

Em-dashes (banned)

COMP SAYS

Never use — ; use a period or nothing (“an AI tell”).

SITE DOES

notify.ts email subject/headline + toast “…any town.” (3264) still use em-dashes.

Drift: Residual em-dashes in owner-alert email + one toast.

Banned names

COMP SAYS

Product = Check It For Me · AI = Check AI · Staff · currency = a check. Never CheckIt / Runnr / Fungibles.

SITE DOES

notify.ts still sends sender “CheckIt” and a “Runnr” email footer.

Drift: Legacy owner-alert path ships banned names.

Two email systems

COMP SAYS

One voice per event.

SITE DOES

alerts.ts ships two divergent template sets (plain DEFAULT_TEMPLATES vs branded EMAIL_DESIGN) for the same events.

Drift: Consolidate to one system to stop copy drift.

Spine / rename (approved)

✅ owner-approved divergence
COMP SAYS

Comp: “No answer = no charge. You always get the verdict.” · “Fungibles” · “Fungie+” · “Powered by {brand} · 1 check used”.

SITE DOES

Site: “No answer = no charge.” · “Check It For Me” · “Check+” · “1 check used”.

Drift: All four are your signed-off copy changes.

Gaps (next pass)

Final verdict page + “couldn’t tell” poll (simulated call didn’t resolve in the capture window), the calls-by-day pop-over (#calbtn never became clickable), and the comp frames as pixel images (the comp .dc.html is a presentation deck that won’t self-render). All verdict/poll copy and tokens for these are documented from source in the drift list.
